rules_fired.because: DEPC carbamoylates primary amines, including the amine group in Tris buffer. Adding DEPC to Tris-containing solutions does not decontaminate them — it simply inactivates DEPC while leaving RNases untouched. Always prepare DEPC-treated water first (0.1% DEPC in nuclease-free water, stir 1 h), then use that water to prepare Tris or other buffers afterward.
rules_fired.cite: Sambrook J & Russell DW (2001) Molecular Cloning 3rd ed., Appendix 1 (DEPC treatment). Ambion RNase decontamination guide.

rules_fired.because: Residual DEPC in treated water carbamoylates adenosine residues in RNA, destroying yield and introducing A260 artefacts. Autoclaving (121°C, 15 min) hydrolyses DEPC completely to CO₂ and ethanol — both volatile and harmless. Do not use DEPC-treated solutions in RNA reactions without autoclaving first. DEPC stock hydrolyses on contact with water; once opened, activity drops within months even in a sealed bottle. Aliquot and store at 4°C.
rules_fired.cite: Chomczynski P (1993) BioTechniques 15:532–534 (TRIzol/DEPC rationale). Sigma-Aldrich DEPC Product Info 40718.
